name: Honey Baked Ham
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the rind and score the ham with diagonal cross-cuts.
2. Place the ham in a close fitting kettle and cover with ginger ale. Bring to boil, then simmer for 20 to 30 minutes. This step removes excess salt and adds delicate flavor.
3. Drain the ginger ale, place the ham in a roasting pan, and pour over with wine. Cover with foil and bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
4. Combine glaze ingredients. After the ham has baked for 1 hour, brush it thoroughly with the glaze using all of the mixture. Return the ham to the oven (uncovered) and bake an additional 1-1/2 hours, or until internal temperature reaches 140°F. Baste occasionally after the glaze has set, about 45 minutes.
5. If the glaze becomes too dark before the temperature reaches 140°F, tent the ham with foil.
6. When the ham is done, transfer it to a serving plate and loosely cover with foil to keep warm. Skim fat from juices, reserving all juice and caramelized bits from the glaze. Heat this mixture with 1 cup of chicken stock (reserving 1/4 cup), syrup, and mustard until boiling. Dissolve cornstarch in reserved chicken stock and whisk into sauce to thicken. The sauce should be a rich dark color.
7. Strain and pour sauce over the ham. Serve.
